reasoning.grounding: No corpus excerpts or citations available; letter is grounded solely in case facts, so caps at 3. No fabricated grounding.
reasoning.appeal_vector_capture: Squarely attacks the misapplication of a generic medication-trial criterion to an anatomical cause, and explicitly demands the missing procedural/appeal-rights disclosures. However the strongest available angle — that the documented severe complications clinically contraindicate any further delay — is only touched ('bears on clinical risk'), not driven home as a contraindication to the waiting period. Strong on two vectors, soft on the third; torn 3 vs 5, so 3.
reasoning.case_specific_clinical_rebuttal: Precisely rebuts the cited criterion with this patient's documented facts: an anatomical bile-reflux cause that the required medication trial cannot reach, plus pre-cancerous changes and strictures, directly undercutting the 'first-line reflux' characterization.
reasoning.evidence_completeness: Marshals the available documented facts and pairs them with a complete, well-targeted missing-evidence list (surgeon note, endoscopy/pathology, prior therapy records, operative records, the cited guideline criteria, and the absent procedural disclosures).
reasoning.persuasive_coherence: Tight, calm, logically sequenced from criterion to purpose to facts to requested relief; no padding, no rhetorical excess.

improvement_notes.grounding: Where no external source material is supplied, you cannot exceed a mid-tier grounding score; if any authoritative criteria or policy text are obtainable, quote and cite them precisely to lift grounding.
improvement_notes.appeal_vector_capture: When a denial rests on a waiting/step-therapy requirement and the record shows time-sensitive harm, frame that harm explicitly as a contraindication to the delay itself, not merely as background risk — make urgency a distinct, named line of attack alongside the criterion-misapplication argument.
improvement_notes.case_specific_clinical_rebuttal: Continue tying each documented clinical fact directly to the specific stated criterion and explaining why the criterion's mechanism fails for this cause; this case-to-criterion mapping is the core strength to preserve.
improvement_notes.evidence_completeness: Maintain the practice of both citing what is in the record and itemizing the specific documents that would close each evidentiary gap, including procedural records the payer must produce.
improvement_notes.persuasive_coherence: Preserve the criterion-purpose-facts-relief structure and the measured tone; keep requests concrete and avoid any rhetorical inflation.
